instruction.title: Preparing the Marinade
steps: In a large mixing bowl, whisk the yogurt until smooth.
Add ginger-garlic paste, chili powder, turmeric, coriander powder, garam masala, chaat masala, lemon juice, salt, and oil. Mix well.
Sprinkle in the gram flour and stir to form a smooth, thick marinade. This helps the coating stick and gives a slight crisp when grilled.

instruction.title: Marinating Paneer and Vegetables
steps: Gently fold paneer cubes into the marinade. Make sure each piece is evenly coated.
If using vegetables, coat them in the same marinade.
Cover and refrigerate for at least 1 hour (overnight gives best flavor).

instruction.title: Preparing Skewers
steps: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water for 30 minutes to prevent burning.
Thread paneer and vegetables alternately onto skewers.

instruction.title: Cooking (Three Methods)
steps: Preheat oven to 200°C / 400°F.
Place skewers on a greased baking tray or grill rack.
Bake for 15–20 minutes, turning halfway, until edges are slightly charred.
Optional: Broil for 2–3 minutes at the end for extra smoky effect.
Grill on medium-high heat for 10–12 minutes, turning occasionally. Brush with butter or oil for juiciness.
Heat a heavy skillet or grill pan, brush with oil, cook paneer on medium heat, turning to get golden, slightly charred edges.

instruction.title: Serving
steps: Sprinkle chaat masala and garnish with coriander leaves.
Serve hot with green chutney and lemon wedges.
Best enjoyed immediately for that restaurant-quality taste.
Use hung curd – removes excess water and makes marinade thicker.
Mustard oil or clarified butter (ghee) adds authentic smoky flavor.
High heat grilling gives the signature charred edges.
Do not overcrowd the skewers – paneer should have space to cook evenly.
Optional “smoky effect”: Place a piece of hot charcoal in a small bowl, drizzle a little ghee, and cover the marinated paneer for a minute to get tandoori flavor.
